original_abstract: Carbohelicenes are iconic polycyclic aromatic compounds whose unique helical topology imparts significant chiroptical properties, such as circularly polarized luminescence (CPL), making them highly attractive for advanced optical applications. While significant strides have been made in the enantioselective synthesis of larger or fused helicene derivatives, a direct catalytic approach to lower, non-fused carbo[n]helicenes (n = 4–6) has remained elusive. Here, we present a general and efficient synthesis of these lower carbohelicenes via palladium(0)-catalyzed C–H arylation. The methodology utilizes a specialized chiral bifunctional phosphine-carboxylate ligand to achieve high yields and excellent enantioselectivities (up to 98:2 e.r.) from achiral precursors. Under the developed conditions, we demonstrate the construction of various configurationally stable carbo[4]-, [5]-, and [6]helicenes, as well as an azahelicene analogue. Computational analysis using density functional theory suggests that the stereochemical outcome is dictated by a complex interplay of C–H activation and reductive elimination, with non-covalent interactions and steric factors between the ligand and substrate playing a decisive role. Furthermore, a systematic evaluation of the photophysical and chiroptical properties reveals substantial dissymmetry factors for these smaller congeners, particularly carbo[4]helicenes, providing new insights into the design of chiroptical materials.
humanized_abstract: Carbohelicenes are well-known chiral polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (CPAHs) with a helical structure that often exhibit interesting chiroptical properties including circularly polarized luminescence (CPL). Synthesis of larger enantiomerically-enriched helicene derivatives including their fused analogues have been reported in literature. To the best of our knowledge, direct catalytic synthesis of lower non-fused carbo[n]helicenes (n = 4–6) has not been reported so far. We here present palladium(0) catalyzed C–H arylation to produce lower carbohelicenes efficiently. With the chiral bifunctional phosphine-carboxylate ligand is shown to catalyze the C–H arylation reaction of achiral precursors to yield the corresponding carbohelicenes with e.r. up to 98:2. The efficient synthesis of various lower carbohelicenes including carbo[4]-, [5]-, and [6]helicenes and azahelicene derivatives has been reported. DFT calculation revealed that the catalytic stereochemistry is controlled by the C–H activation/reductive elimination process which is in turn highly dependent on the non-covalent interactions between the ligand and the substrate and the steric interactions between them. The photophysical and chiroptical studies were carried out in detail and it is observed that the dissymmetry factor of the smaller congeners like carbo[4]helicenes are quite large which opens up new avenues for designing chiroptical materials.
